name: Prescience
permanentEffect: At the end of your turn,if you have not passed, place 1 of the plan cards on your faction sheet, face down. At the start of your turn, reveal and resolve the plan card on your faction sheet, if able

name: The Starlit Path
window: At the end of your turn
windowEffect: At the end of your turn, you may spend 5 path tokens to resolve the secondary ability of any strategy card, or 8 path tokens to do so without spending a command token.

name: Narrow Way
windowEffect: At any time, you may spend 1 path token to redistribute 1 of your command tokens and move 2 of your infantry on the game board onto a planet you control.

name: Ancient Empire
permanentEffect: During setup, place 1 tomb token on each of up to 14 planets. When you gain control of a planet that contains a tomb token, purge that token to capture 2 infantry from the supply.

name: Mark Of Pharadn
permanentEffect: Abilities that would allow you to place infantry on the game board instead allow you to produce those units. When you produce infantry, you must return 1 of your captured infantry to produce each of those units, instead of spending resources.

name: Data Recovery
permanentEffect: Once per action, after 1 or more of your units is destroyed, you may place 1 of the active player's control tokens on your faction sheet.

name: Tempest Squads
permanentEffect: While you are the active player, 2 infantry in the active system do not count against your ships' capacity.
notes: Think of this as sort of like a mild N'orr hero.

name: Black Ops
window: After you win a combat as the attacker
windowEffect: You may remove 3 of your opponent's control tokens from your faction sheet to gain 1 command token, draw 2 action cards, or draw 1 secret objective

name: Synthesis
permanentEffect: Readied planets you control have PLANETARY SHIELD and PRODUCTION 0 as if they were units. Apply +1 to the PRODUCTION value of each of your units with PRODUCTION.

name: Living Metals
permanentEffect: Agenda outcomes and action cards that have component actions cannot remove or destroy your units, or place other player's units on planets you control.

name: Lost Crown of Kaltrim
permanentEffect: After setup, distribute 1 shrine attachment and token to each other player at random, including the Kaltrim Crown token. Each of those players must attach that shrine to a planet in a system adjacent to their home system or the Mecatol Rex system.

name: Raider Coves
permanentEffect: When you commit units to a planet another player controls, you may choose for your units to coexist; if you do, gain 2 trade goods.

name: Evasive
permanentEffect: Your units do not exit coexistence unless another player ends coexistence with those units. Other players must spend 2 influence to end coexistence with your units on a planet.

name: Matters of State
permanentEffect: After you perform an action or resolve an action card, gain 1 balance token or flip 1 balance token on your faction sheet.

name: Intrigue
permanentEffect: At the end of a player's turn, you may spend 1 token from your strategy pool and 1 Intrigue card's listed balance token cost; resolve that card's ability. Then, place 1 infantry from your reinforcements into coexistence on a non-home planet the active player controls to gain 1 command token.

name: Harmony
permanentEffect: If you have more balance tokens of one type than the other, flip your faction technologies and mech to the matching side; the other side has no effect and your commodity value is 2.

name: Mutineers
windowEffect: Place 1 token from your strategy pool in a non-home system that contains exactly 1 of another player's ships to replace that ship with a neutral unit of the same type.

name: Questing Prince
permanentEffect: When you gain control of a planet that has a shrine attachment, purge that attachment. When you would purge the Kaltrim Crown token, instead place it on your faction sheet and gain 1 victory point.

name: Shrines
permanentEffect: Shrine attachments do not count toward scoring objectives and their tokens can only be looked at or revealed by your abilities.

name: Classified Developments
permanentEffect: ACTION: Spend a combined total of 5 resources or influence to place 1 superweapon on a planet you control, and gain its superweapon card.

name: Superweapon Projects
permanentEffect: Superweapons you control are structures and cannot be destroyed by abilities. Each planet can contain a maximum of 1 superweapon. When a superweapon is destroyed, instead purge it and its superweapon card.

name: Honor-Bound
permanentEffect: When you gain honor, place or flip 1 code token on your faction sheet face up, if able. When you gain dishonor, instead place or flip 1 code token face down, if able.

name: Pride
permanentEffect: When you win a combat as the attacker, gain 1 honor if your opponent has more victory points than you, or gain 1 dishonor if they have fewer victory points than you; otherwise, gain 1 honor or 1 dishonor and ignore this ability until the end of your turn.

name: The Code
permanentEffect: When you gain—or lose—honor or dishonor, gain or lose the Code cards corresponding with your current honor and dishonor. This faction has the abilities on those Code cards.

name: Bestow (2 Honor)
permanentEffect: After you replenish commodities, gain 2 commodities or convert up to 2 of your commodities to trade goods.

name: Reflect (5 Honor)
permanentEffect: After you draw 1 or more action cards, draw 1 action card and give 1 action card from your hand to one of your neighbors.

name: Ascend (8 Honor)
permanentEffect: When you gain this card, gain 1 victory point. When you lose this card, lose 1 victory point.

name: Thwart (2 Dishonor)
permanentEffect: After you replenish commodities, take up to 2 commodities from any combination of your neighbors.
notes: Not automated, have your neighbors subtract with `/player stats`.

name: Deceive (5 Dishonor)
permanentEffect: After you draw 1 or more action cards, take 1 action card at random from one of your neighbors.

name: Scourge (8 Dishonor)
permanentEffect: Purge your promissory notes; you cannot gain honor. You treat scored secret objectives as public objectives, instead of public objectives.
notes: This also purges promissory notes in other players' play areas and hands.
